title: A Reporter's Story from Tehran During Attacks
summary: A reporter named Reza Sayah is in Tehran, Iran, and he tells us what is happening there. For three days, the U.S. and Israel have been attacking Iran with airstrikes. Reza hears loud jet fighters and big explosions that shake the ground. The attacks started at midnight and lasted for 90 minutes. The Internet was cut off, so Reza cannot use it to send messages. He talks on the phone instead. The strikes are hitting military buildings, but also non-military places like hospitals, schools, and homes. Reza visited a police station that was destroyed, and nearby houses were damaged too. He met a man in a cafe who saw a scary video of someone hurt during an attack. The man described two explosions close together. U.S. President Trump said Iran wants to talk, but he also warned of more strikes. The situation is very tense and dangerous for people in Tehran.

keywords.explanation: big, loud bangs that can destroy things
a way to connect to computers and send information online
the army or soldiers who protect a country
a feeling of worry or fear because something bad might happen
broken or hurt, not working properly

questions.question: Who is the reporter in Tehran?
How many days have the airstrikes been happening?
What did Reza hear during the attacks?
Why is Reza talking on the phone instead of using the Internet?
What kind of places are being hit besides military targets?
What did the man in the cafe describe?

background_read: This article is about a reporter in Tehran, Iran, during a time of conflict. Iran is a country in the Middle East, and it is being attacked by the U.S. and Israel. Airstrikes are attacks from airplanes that drop bombs, causing explosions. The reporter describes how these attacks affect people, including damage to buildings and loss of Internet access. It is important to understand that wars can be scary and dangerous, and reporters help us learn what is happening in other parts of the world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Reza Sayah, a reporter in Tehran, Iran
WHAT: He describes airstrikes by the U.S. and Israel causing explosions and damage
WHERE: In Tehran, the capital city of Iran
WHY: To report on the tense and dangerous conditions during the attacks

perspectives.description: Reza shares what he sees and hears on the ground, showing the impact on people and places.
